sdo:itemDesc.dc:title: Handwritten postcard from Oppel to Schenker, postmarked May 1 or 2, 1932
sdo:itemDesc.dc:description: Oppel notifies Schenker of a forthcoming radio broadcast of his Wind Serenade.

tei:address.tei:addrLine: An: Herrn Prof Dr. Heinrich Schenker

tei:opener.tei:salute: Mein lieber Herr Professor Schenker,Receipt of this postcard is not recorded in Schenker’s diary.

tei:div.tei:p: am 11. Mai vormittags 10 bringt der Rdfk Mannheim meine Bläserserenade. Vielleicht können Sie sie hören? –

In d. theoretischen Prüfung bei uns legte ich einer Schülerin Chopin vor, Mazurka op 592, insbesondere Takt 77–89, sie fand sich sehr gut ab damit.The passage in question begins in the home key of the Mazurka, A major, but appears to pass through a great number of keys in the foreground, i.e. on the surface of the music, in ever greater frequency before finding its way back to A in bar 87 and making a perfect cadence in that key two bars later. (The point of the exercise is to show that a mechanical Roman-numeral analysis of the harmony does not help us understand the passage; only the part-writing (voice-leading) can clarify it. Schenker himself gives an example of such a passage in a remark appended to his graph of Chopin’s Etude in C minor, Op. 10, No. 12, published in the Five Analyses in Sketchform (1932).) –
Bepo geht’s gut, ich zeige ihm im Wald jetzt Vögel u. Eichhörnchen. –

30. IV. Grade kam Ihre l. Karte,Schenker’s diary for April 28 records: “An Oppel (K.): bestätige die Sendung, erbitte die Walzer zurück.” (“To Oppel (postcard): I confirm the arrival of his package, I ask him to return the waltzes.”). — On May 9 Schenker’s diary records: “An Oppel (Br.): Dank für die Walzer, die alten u. neuen; sonstige Nachrichten: Furtwängler, Weisse, Brockhaus usw.” (“To Oppel (letter): thanks for the waltzes, the old and the new. Other news: Furtwängler, Weisse, Brockhaus, etc.”). vielen Dank! In der kommenden Woche schicke ich auch die Walzer zurück, Frau Schenker hat ganz Recht, mich zu mahnen.
Bei uns wird Hänsel u. Gretel aufgeführt, aber diese ewigen Terzmodulationen Humperdincks gehen mir auf die Nerven.
Was macht Weisse? Kommt er im Sommer zurück? –
On May 11 at 10 a.m., Mannheim Radio is broadcasting my Wind Serenade. Perhaps you can listen to it? –
In our theory examination, I gave a female student of mine Chopin’s Mazurka Op. 59, No. 2, especially bars 77–89, and she handled it very well. –

Last week I had dealings with the head of department at the Town Hall, regarding the salary issue; for myself I learned at least that the full 35% reduction would not be applied until next semester. –
Bepo is doing well; I am now pointing birds and squirrels out to him. –
On April 30 your kind postcard arrived; many thanks! In the coming week I will also return the waltzes. Mrs Schenker has every right to admonish me.
Hansel and Gretel is being performed here, but Humperdinck’s eternal modulations by thirds get on my nerves.
What’s happening with Weisse? Is he returning in the summer?

tei:closer.tei:salute: to the two of you from all of us
